executiveSummary.text: US-China trade tensions remain elevated with 145% tariffs in effect as of April 10; markets pricing in delayed Fed cuts into late-2026 due to war-related inflation from US-Iran standoff over Strait of Hormuz
S&P 500 rose 0.3% Friday (April 24) to ~7,109; Nasdaq +0.7%; both hit all-time highs before pulling back Thursday. AI/semiconductor leadership intact but momentum slowing
Crude oil at $95.13 WTI / $104.63 Brent (April 24) — elevated 51% YoY on Iran supply risk. Gold ~$4,707/oz, silver ~$75.8. Energy/inflation tradeoff tightening Fed's hands
STI crosses 4,700 for first time; DBS at record S$57.92, OCBC at S$20.19. Singapore banks leading the index higher on constructive 2026 outlook. Local banks now >50% of STI weight
Fed's preferred inflation gauge (PCE) now expected at 3.7% Q2, 3.4% Q3, 3.2% Q4 2026 — ~30bps above prior expectations. Rate cut timeline pushed to late-2026 at earliest

regime.label: Stagflationary Expansion — Elevated Inflation + Moderate Growth + Tight Financial Conditions

metrics.label: Fed Funds Rate
metrics.change: Held Jan 2026; cut pushed to late 2026

usGlobal.what: AI semiconductor sector paused 13-day Nasdaq streak — AVGO, NVDA, ASML, AMAT lead pullback from all-time highs
usGlobal.why: Overextended positioning; QQQ held but momentum fading. AI capex thesis intact but valuation stretched near-term
usGlobal.tickers: NVDA, AVGO, ASML, AMAT, LRCX, KLAC, QQQ
usGlobal.implication: AI Infra (420k SGD, 16%) faces short-term headwind. Hold positions; watch for re-entry opportunity if pullback >5%

usGlobal.what: Fed rate cut timeline pushed back to late-2026 on war-related inflation (Reuters poll, Apr 22)
usGlobal.why: PCE now 3.7% Q2, 3.4% Q3, 3.2% Q4 — 30bps above prior forecast. Iran tensions driving energy price surge
usGlobal.implication: Real rates likely to rise even if nominal holds. Gold's real-rate hedge dynamic complex — elevated rates structurally negative for zero-yield gold. Cash and short-duration better positioned than long-duration bonds

usGlobal.what: US-China tariffs at 145% on advanced semiconductors, EVs, solar as of April 10, 2026
usGlobal.why: Tariffs failed to change Beijing's trade/military policy (Reuters, Apr 21). Supreme Court Feb 20 ruled President cannot use IEEPA for universal tariffs; admin pivoted to other authority
usGlobal.implication: TSM and ASML face China revenue exposure risk. Scarcity thesis on semis STRENGTHENED — supply chain diversification accelerates, benefiting AMAT, LRCX, KLAC in non-China markets

usGlobal.what: Crude oil elevated at $95 WTI / $105 Brent on US-Iran Hormuz tensions — 51-59% YoY gain
usGlobal.why: Strait of Hormuz threat directly impacts ~20% of global oil flow. War risk premium embedded in price. Transportation/inflation pass-through ongoing
usGlobal.implication: Energy equities (EWY has exposure) benefit but inflation feedthrough hurts consumer. 2nd order: sustained high energy prices → demand destruction →反向 pressure on crude

singapore.what: STI crosses 4,700 for first time; DBS at record S$57.92, OCBC at all-time high S$20.19 (Apr 22-25)
singapore.why: Singapore banks outperforming on strong NIM outlook, digital banking leadership, and constructive 2026 economic environment. Together >50% of STI
singapore.implication: SG Div (442k SGD, 17%) heavily exposed to bank concentration risk. OCBC and DBS at all-time highs = rich valuations. UOB slightly behind — better risk/reward for new entry

singapore.what: STI target raised to 4,800 on Bloomberg consensus; OCBC Research sees 'constructive' 2026 environment
singapore.why: Carmen Lee (OCBC) cites benign global growth, strong financials. STI at record highs but not stretched on forward P/E
singapore.implication: STI passive position (VWRA) benefits. Active tilt toward UOB over DBS/OCBC at current prices

singapore.what: REITs under pressure as interest rate outlook delays Fed cuts; Ascendas, CICT, MLT, FCT valuations compressed
singapore.why: Higher-for-longer rate environment compresses REIT P/NAV multiples. Cap rates rising. Rental income support but capital values pressured
singapore.implication: REIT allocation (part of 442k SG Div) faces headwind. Yield spread vs risk-free narrowing. Prefer Singtel, Keppel, Sembcorp over REITs in SG Div bucket

commodities.what: Gold at $4,707/oz (April 24) — up 0.33% but REAL RATE HEADWIND intensifying as Fed delays cuts
commodities.why: Gold is a REAL RATE hedge, NOT a crisis hedge. When real rates rise (nominal rates hold while inflation rises), gold is structurally pressured. Current dynamic: inflation rising faster than nominal rates can be cut
commodities.implication: Metals allocation (175k SGD, 7%) faces 2nd-order pressure. Gold held as portfolio insurance but expect volatility. Silver more industrial — benefits from AI/energy transition capex. Copper benefits from AI infrastructure buildout. If gold breaks below $4,640 support, could trigger cascade

commodities.what: Crude at $95 WTI — energy sector elevated but demand destruction risk emerging at these price levels
commodities.why: 2nd order: $95+ oil → input cost inflation for manufacturers → margin compression → demand destruction fear. Transportation, airlines, chemical sectors most exposed
commodities.implication: Energy sector in EWY and GRID beneficial near-term but monitor for demand destruction threshold (~$100+ sustained = demand erosion)

commodities.what: Uranium sector quiet but structural deficit thesis intact — nuclear power demand rising globally
commodities.why: No major news in past 24h but multi-year uranium supply deficit unchanged. Data center nuclear power agreements accelerating
commodities.implication: GRID (uranium) hold. No new catalyst in next 2-4 weeks without geopolitical escalation

narratives.title: US-Iran Standoff / Hormuz Risk Premium
narratives.description: US-Iran tensions continue driving energy risk premium. Supply shock inflation dynamic — Fed cannot cut. Oil elevated on supply disruption fears

narratives.title: AI Infrastructure Capex Supercycle — Pausing, Not Ending
narratives.description: 13-day Nasdaq streak broken. AI trade overstretched near-term but QQQ/NVDA/AVGO/ASML thesis intact. Hyperscaler earnings to clarify AI capex trajectory. Tariffs on semis accelerate non-China supply chain buildout
narratives.tickers: NVDA, AVGO, ASML, AMAT, LRCX, KLAC, QQQ

narratives.title: Fed Trapped — Can't Cut, Won't Hike
narratives.description: Fed holds at 3.5-3.75% but inflation expectations ratchet higher. PCE 3.7% Q2 vs 3.4% prior. Real rates effectively positive and rising. 'Two-sided' framing — hike possible if Iran escalation worsens. Market pricing late-2026 cut at earliest

narratives.title: Singapore Banks at Records — Concentration Risk in STI
narratives.description: DBS and OCBC at all-time highs pushing STI to 4,700+. Banks >50% of index. Constructive fundamentals but valuations pricing in perfection. Rising NIM from sustained high rates but credit cycle risk underappreciated

narratives.title: China Tariffs — 145% Effectively a Full Trade Stop
narratives.description: US-China trade at effective standstill for advanced goods. Supreme Court Feb 20 ruled IEEPA can't be used for universal tariffs. Companies seeking refunds via CAPE portal (April 20). China retaliation in progress. Supply chain diversification accelerating

secondOrderEffects.text: Oil $95+ → Energy input inflation → PCE stays elevated at 3.7% Q2 → Fed can't cut → Real rates rise → Gold gets crushed (NOT 'gold up in crisis'). Gold's 3rd-order loser in this specific scenario: real assets vs cash/short duration
secondOrderEffects.implication: Metals allocation (175k SGD, 7%) faces structural headwind. Consider reducing gold exposure vs silver/copper which have industrial demand catalysts

secondOrderEffects.text: Iran Hormuz threat → Oil spike → Shipping/transport costs rise → Global supply chain cost pressure → Margin compression for manufacturers → Demand destruction fear → Reverse pressure on oil at $100+ sustained. Self-limiting equilibrium at these price levels
secondOrderEffects.implication: Energy positions in EWY/GRID: take profits on spikes rather than adding. Monitor crude demand destruction signals

secondOrderEffects.text: STI concentration in banks at all-time highs → If banks correct, STI drawdown amplified → VWRA exposure concentrated. STI 4,800 target based on Bloomberg consensus — crowded trade
secondOrderEffects.implication: Diversify SG Div away from bank-heavy STI toward Singtel, Keppel, Sembcorp at better valuations. UOB less extended than DBS/OCBC

secondOrderEffects.text: US-China 145% tariffs on chips → TSM/ASML China revenue at risk → Accelerate non-China capex (US, Taiwan, Europe, Japan, India) → AMAT, LRCX, KLAC benefit from non-China AI infrastructure buildout — counterintuitively POSITIVE for KL's semi equipment holdings
secondOrderEffects.implication: Scarcity thesis STRENGTHENED. AI Infra allocation (420k SGD, 16%) in AMAT/LRCX/KLAC more resilient than NVDA/AVGO which face direct China demand questions

secondOrderEffects.text: Fed trapped → US yield curve steepens → Regional bank stress (NIM compression on variable rate loans) → Credit cycle risk rises in 2026 H2. Singapore bank NIM currently strong but credit cycle reversal could snap quickly
secondOrderEffects.implication: DBS/OCBC/UOB at records — protect gains with stop-loss discipline. Do NOT add at all-time highs. Credit cycle risk underpriced in current valuations

investment.thesis: UOB preferred over DBS/OCBC for Singapore bank exposure — UOB less extended, better risk/reward at current prices
investment.trigger: STI pullback >3% or UOB underperforms DBS/OCBC on next earnings
investment.whyNow: DBS at S$57.92 and OCBC at S$20.19 — all-time highs price in perfection. UOB still has upside to consensus target
investment.invalidator: Singapore credit cycle deterioration, property market slump, or NIM compression from rate cuts (unlikely in 2026)

investment.thesis: Non-China semiconductor equipment (AMAT, LRCX, KLAC) better risk/reward than NVDA/AVGO near-term
investment.trigger: TSM earnings guidance or AMAT/LRCX next earnings beat
investment.whyNow: US-China 145% tariffs accelerate non-China AI fab buildout. AMAT/LRCX/KLAC supply fab equipment — no China revenue dependency, pure AI infrastructure beneficiary
investment.invalidator: AI capex cycle ends, hyperscaler spending cuts, or new China tariff exemptions that disadvantage allies

trading.thesis: Reduce gold exposure on rallies toward $4,750-4,800 — real rate headwind and Fed delay structurally negative
trading.whyNow: Real rates rising (nominal flat, inflation rising). Fed can't cut to help gold. Dollar likely strengthens. Gold 3rd-order loser in oil-shock inflation scenario
trading.invalidator: Iran escalation triggers safe-haven bid overwhelming real-rate headwind; $100+ oil breaks inflation expectations loose

trading.thesis: Take partial profits on energy/EWY on crude spikes above $98 — demand destruction threshold nearby
trading.whyNow: $95+ sustained → 2nd order demand destruction likely emerges in 2-4 months. Energy equities pricing in $100+ scenarios. Better to take profits at elevated prices than chase higher
trading.invalidator: Iran escalation goes kinetic — Strait of Hormuz actually disrupted. Energy spike becomes self-fulfilling but economically destructive

watchlist.thesis: NVDA Q2 earnings guidance — AI capex sustainability test. Hyperscaler miss = AI trade unwinds
watchlist.whyNow: AI Infra 420k SGD thesis requires AI capex cycle to continue. Tariff war adds cost uncertainty. Cloud spend guidance will set sector tone for months

watchlist.thesis: Fed official speeches late April — 'two-sided' framing could hint at hike if Iran escalation worsens
watchlist.whyNow: FOMC minutes showed officials flagging hike possibility if inflation worsens. Iran/oil shock is the test case. Any hawkish deviation = bond market repricing

riskWatch.name: Iran Geopolitical Oil Shock
riskWatch.description: US-Iran standoff over Strait of Hormuz could drive crude to $100-120/bbl if kinetic. Would create stagflationary shock — input inflation surges, Fed trapped, real rates spike, gold crushed, growth fears. Most asymmetric risk for KL's portfolio
riskWatch.mitigation: Reduce gold on rallies. Energy/EWY positions sized appropriately — don't overweight. Short-duration bonds and cash preferred. Monitor crude demand destruction signals

riskWatch.name: Fed Rate Path Repricing
riskWatch.description: Market currently pricing late-2026 cut. If PCE remains above 3.5% or Iran escalation worsens, cut timeline could be pushed further or hikes re-enter debate. Would reprice ALL risk assets simultaneously
riskWatch.mitigation: Maintain 26% cash buffer. Avoid long-duration bonds (TLT). Short-duration and variable rate instruments better positioned

riskWatch.name: Singapore Bank Concentration / Credit Cycle
riskWatch.description: DBS, OCBC, UOB at or near all-time highs = maximum sentiment. Together >50% of STI. Credit cycle reversal could cause sharp de-rating. KL's 17% SG Div heavily exposed
riskWatch.mitigation: Rotate SG Div from bank-heavy STI toward Singtel (defensive yield), Keppel/Sembcorp. Reduce marginal DBS/OCBC exposure

riskWatch.name: AI Trade Unwind / Capex Cycle Peak
riskWatch.description: 13-day Nasdaq streak ended. AI valuations stretched. NVDA trades at extreme premium. Any guidance disappointment could trigger 10-15% sector correction
riskWatch.mitigation: AI Infra (16%) in diversified semi equipment names (AMAT, LRCX, KLAC) more resilient than pure GPU plays. QQQ as diversified anchor

riskWatch.name: US-China Trade Escalation — 3rd Order
riskWatch.description: 145% tariffs effectively standstill on advanced goods. China retaliation ongoing. Could expand to financial sector or supply chain actions. Third-country allies caught in crossfire
riskWatch.mitigation: TSM, ASML have China exposure — monitor. AMAT/LRCX/KLAC non-China capex beneficiaries. Diversification thesis strengthened

keyEvents.text: S&P 500 +0.3%, Nasdaq +0.7% Friday (Apr 24) — 13-day streak broken, records set before pullback
WTI crude $95.13/bbl (-0.76%), Brent $104.63 (-0.42%) — elevated on Iran/Hormuz risk
Gold $4,707/oz (+0.33% Apr 24) — real rate headwind building despite geopolitical premium
Fed rate cut pushed to late-2026; PCE inflation now 3.7% Q2 (30bps above prior) — Reuters poll Apr 22
STI crosses 4,700; DBS S$57.92 (+2.2%) record, OCBC S$20.19 (+1.2%) all-time high
US-China 145% tariffs on advanced chips, EVs, solar — in effect since April 10, 2026
Supreme Court Feb 20 ruled IEEPA can't support universal tariffs; CAPE portal opened Apr 20 for refund claims
FOMC Jan 2026: held at 3.5-3.75%; two members dissented wanting cut. March minutes: inflation 'somewhat elevated'

whatMatters: Iran/Hormuz oil risk — can spike crude to $100+ and trap Fed; this is THE macro variable
Fed cut timeline pushed to late-2026 — real rates rising; gold is a real-rate hedge NOT a crisis hedge — don't be misled
STI/bank concentration at extremes — protect gains, don't add at all-time highs
AI semi equipment (AMAT/LRCX/KLAC) benefits from China tariff-driven diversification — counterintuitive POSITIVE
whatToDo: Hold 26% cash — dry powder for corrections. Don't rush into equity highs
Rotate SG Div: reduce DBS/OCBC (at records), add UOB (less extended), Singtel/Keppel/Sembcorp
Reduce gold on rallies toward $4,750 — real rate headwind too strong. Prefer silver/copper for metals exposure
Watch NVDA earnings guidance (late May) and any hawkish Fed speaker — these set next moves
whatToIgnore: Daily S&P/Nasdaq point moves — noise when at all-time highs. Focus on regime shifts not noise
'Crisis = gold up' heuristic — LAZY and WRONG. In inflationary oil shock, gold gets crushed. Always trace the causal chain
STI 4,800 Bloomberg consensus target — this is a crowded late-cycle trade. Price targets are rearview mirrors
